title: manulea tomentosa
intro: Manulea tomentosa, also known as the hairy manulea, is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of its host plant. The medicinal properties of Manulea tomentosa include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infusion, which is used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
